steps.title: Find the median position
steps.content: With 17 restaurants, the median is the middle value when arranged in order. For 17 values, the median is at position $\frac{17+1}{2} = 9$.

steps.title: Build cumulative frequencies to locate the 9th value
steps.content:
Count restaurants cumulatively through each range:
  • Through 2–7 employees: 2
  • Through 8–13 employees: 2 + 4 = 6
  • Through 14–19 employees: 6 + 2 = 8
  • Through 20–25 employees: 8 + 7 = 15
Since 8 restaurants are accounted for through the 14–19 range and 15 through the 20–25 range, the 9th restaurant falls in the 20–25 employee group.

steps.title: Identify the viable choice
steps.content: The median must lie between 20 and 25. Checking the choices: 2 is in the 2–7 range, 9 is in the 8–13 range, 15 is in the 14–19 range, and 21 is in the 20–25 range. Only 21 falls in the correct range. That matches choice D — D is correct.
